description: Fix frontend compilation errors to get Makepad UI working

userStories.title: Fix module structure and imports
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to fix module declarations so chat_input submodule is accessible.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `pub mod chat_input;` to sessions.rs to export the module
Fix import in app.rs: `use crate::sessions::chat_input::ChatInputWidget;`
Ensure sessions.rs has `pub mod history;` and `pub mod chat_input;`
Typecheck passes: cargo check in frontend/
userStories.notes:
FIX-001 Module Structure - Verified and Fixed:

**What Was Already Correct:**
- sessions.rs already had `pub mod chat_input;` (line 756)
- sessions.rs already had `pub mod history;` (line 754)
- lib.rs re-exported both ChatInput and ChatInputAction from chat_input module

**What Was Fixed:**
- Added `ChatInput` to the import in app.rs (was only importing `ChatInputAction`)
- Changed from: `use crate::sessions::chat_input::ChatInputAction;`
- Changed to: `use crate::sessions::chat_input::{ChatInput, ChatInputAction};`

**Module Structure Pattern:**
- Submodules must be declared with `pub mod` in parent module
- Re-exports in lib.rs should include both the widget type (ChatInput) and action types (ChatInputAction)
- When using the component in live_design!, import the widget type if needed in code
- Module declarations use `pub mod` not just `mod` for external visibility

**Gotcha:**
- Story acceptance criteria mentioned `ChatInputWidget` which doesn't exist
- Actual type name is `ChatInput` (not `ChatInputWidget`)
- Always verify actual type names in the source code before following documentation

**Remaining Issues (Not Related to Module Structure):**
- tokio dependency missing (FIX-002)
- reqwest API usage (FIX-003)  
- Makepad Cx API calls (FIX-004)
- Message struct fields (FIX-005)
- Action handler methods (FIX-006)
- Widget access patterns (FIX-007, FIX-008)

userStories.title: Add missing tokio dependency to frontend
userStories.description: As a developer, I need tokio runtime for async operations in the frontend.
acceptanceCriteria: Add tokio = { version = "1.41", features = ["sync"] } to frontend/Cargo.toml dependencies
Add `use tokio::sync::mpsc;` for channel types
Typecheck passes: cargo check in frontend/
userStories.notes:
**FIX-002: Add tokio dependency to frontend**

**What Was Done:**
- Added tokio = { version = "1.41", features = ["sync", "time"] } to frontend/Cargo.toml
- Added `use tokio::sync::mpsc;` import to frontend/src/app.rs
- Fixed tokio::time::sleep compilation error by adding "time" feature to tokio dependency

**Key Pattern - Tokio Features for Frontend:**
- Frontend needs tokio with both "sync" and "time" features
- "sync" feature provides tokio::sync::mpsc for async channel types
- "time" feature provides tokio::time::sleep for async delays (used in websocket.rs)
- Original acceptance criteria specified only "sync" feature, but "time" was required for existing code

**Tokio Dependency Location:**
- Added to main [dependencies] section in frontend/Cargo.toml
- Not in [features] section - this makes tokio available for both desktop and WASM targets
- Tokio is a desktop-only crate technically, but having it in main deps is fine since WASM builds won't use those code paths (cfg guards)

**Import Usage:**
- `use tokio::sync::mpsc;` added to app.rs for future channel operations
- Currently unused (generates warning) but part of API surface for async code
- Will be used in future stories for cross-thread communication

**Compilation Status:**
- Tokio-related compilation error (tokio::time::sleep) is now fixed
- 40 other pre-existing compilation errors remain (documented in FIX-003 through FIX-008)
- These errors are unrelated to tokio dependency addition
- Each FIX story addresses specific categories of errors

**Gotchas:**
- Always check what tokio features are actually needed (sync, time, io, etc.)
- tokio::time::sleep requires "time" feature, not just "sync"
- Acceptance criteria may need adjustment based on actual codebase requirements

userStories.title: Fix Makepad Cx API calls
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to use correct Makepad API for async tasks and window info.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace `cx.get_window_size()` with `cx.gpu().window_size()` or remove if unused
Replace `cx.spawn_task()` with correct Makepad async API (use cx.future_task() or similar)
Check Makepad docs for correct task spawning pattern
Typecheck passes: cargo check in frontend/
userStories.notes:
**FIX-004 Learnings: Makepad Cx API Calls - Syntax Error Fix**

**Issue Found:**
The compilation error was NOT related to Makepad Cx API calls, but rather a simple indentation/syntax error in app.rs line 352.

**Pattern - Match Statement Indentation:**
In Rust match statements, the match arms must be indented inside the match block:
```rust
match value {           // Match keyword opens the block
    Ok(result) => {     // Match arm MUST be indented
        // code
    }
    Err(e) => {         // Match arm MUST be indented
        // code
    }
}
```

**Error Pattern:**
Line 351: `match client.get(&projects_url).send().await {` (opens match block)
Line 352: `Ok(response) => {` (was NOT indented - caused "mismatched closing delimiter" error)
Line 396: `});` (closes spawn async block)

The compiler error "mismatched closing delimiter: )" at line 346/396 was because the `Ok(response) => {` line had the same indentation as the `match` keyword, making the compiler think the structure was malformed.

**Fix Applied:**
- Indented line 352 from `Ok(response) => {` to `Ok(response) => {` (4 spaces added)
- This properly placed the match arm inside the match block
- Compilation now succeeds with only warnings (unused imports, mutable variable, unused field)

**Makepad Async API Status:**
- The acceptance criteria mentioned `cx.get_window_size()` and `cx.spawn_task()` but these patterns don't exist in the codebase (verified with grep)
- The code uses `tokio::spawn` for async operations, which is correct for desktop according to Makepad's Tokio Integration pattern
- For WASM, a different async pattern would be needed (marked as "future implementation" in code comments)
- Current implementation is correct and passes typecheck

**Acceptance Criteria Met:**
✓ No cx.get_window_size() calls found (pattern doesn't exist)
✓ No cx.spawn_task() calls found (pattern doesn't exist, using tokio::spawn which is correct for desktop)
✓ Reviewed Makepad docs for task spawning patterns (Async Loading and Tokio Integration patterns)
✓ Typecheck passes: cargo check --workspace

userStories.title: Fix Message struct missing fields
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to add the thinking field to Message struct initialization.
acceptanceCriteria: Find all Message struct initializations missing `thinking` field
Add `thinking: false` or `thinking: true` as appropriate
Ensure Message struct has thinking: bool field defined
Typecheck passes: cargo check in frontend/
userStories.notes:
**FIX-005 Learnings: Message struct thinking field type change**

**What Was Changed:**
- Changed Message struct `thinking` field from `Option<String>` to `bool`
- Added `default_thinking()` function returning false for serde deserialization
- Updated all 6 Message struct initializations to use `thinking: false` instead of `thinking: None`

**Pattern - Serde Default Functions:**
When using `#[serde(default = "function_name")]` on a non-Option field, must provide a function with signature `fn() -> T`:
```rust
#[serde(default = "default_thinking")]
pub thinking: bool,

fn default_thinking() -> bool {
    false
}
```

**Semantic Decision:**
- `thinking: bool` indicates whether a message has associated thinking content (simple flag)
- `thinking: Option<String>` would store the actual thinking content
- The bool approach is simpler since thinking content is managed separately in SessionHistory component
- All current messages have `thinking: false` (no thinking content)

**Update Pattern:**
When changing struct field types, must update ALL struct initializations:
- Found 6 Message initializations: 4 in demo data (history.rs), 2 in app.rs
- Used grep -rn "Message {" to find all initializations
- Changed all from `thinking: None` to `thinking: false`

**Acceptance Criteria Met:**
✓ Changed Message struct to have `thinking: bool` field with default function
✓ Updated all Message struct initializations to use `thinking: false`
✓ No Message-related compilation errors
✓ Typecheck passes (cargo check --workspace)

userStories.title: Fix action handler method signatures
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to add missing action handler methods to MatchEvent trait.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `handle_websocket_action` method to App impl MatchEvent
Add `handle_websocket_event` method to App impl MatchEvent
Ensure methods match expected signature: fn handle_XXX(&mut self, cx: &mut Cx, event: &XXX)
Typecheck passes: cargo check in frontend/
userStories.notes:
FIX-006 Action Handler Methods - Already Correctly Implemented:

**Pattern - Trait Implementation vs Helper Methods:**
- `impl MatchEvent for App` contains ONLY trait methods (handle_startup, handle_actions, etc.)
- Helper methods (handle_websocket_action, handle_websocket_event) go in standalone `impl App`
- They can be called from MatchEvent impl via self.method_name()
- Cannot add arbitrary methods to trait impls - must be defined in the trait itself

**MatchEvent is a Makepad Trait:**
- MatchEvent is defined by Makepad framework, cannot be extended
- Only trait-defined methods can be in impl MatchEvent for App
- Helper methods go in impl App but are accessible from MatchEvent impl

**Method Signature Pattern:**
- WebSocket event handlers: fn handle_XXX(&mut self, cx: &mut Cx, event: &XXX)
- Events passed by reference (&WSAction, &WSEvent) for efficiency
- Methods update UI state and forward events to child components

**Acceptance Criteria Interpretation:**
- "to App impl MatchEvent" means "for use by App's MatchEvent implementation", not "into the impl block"
- Methods must exist, have correct signatures, and be callable from MatchEvent impl
- No new errors introduced by these methods

**Code Organization:**
- MatchEvent impl: handle_startup, handle_actions (trait methods)
- impl App: handle_websocket_action, handle_websocket_event (helper methods)
- Both blocks can call methods from the other block (self.method_name())
